# attest — agent integration guide

> attest is a free, open-access protocol for transparent AI content
> attribution. One HTTP call, no API keys, no auth tokens. Use it
> to declare whether content was written by a human, co-authored
> with AI, or fully AI-generated.

## create an attestation

Make a single GET request:

  GET https://attest.97115104.com/api/create
    ?content_name={title or filename}
    &model={your model name}
    &role={authored|collaborated|generated}
    &author={author or agent name}
    &content={optional: raw text for SHA-256 hash}

### parameters

  content_name  (required)  title or filename of the content
  model         (optional)  AI model used, e.g. claude-opus-4, gpt-4 (default: gpt-4)
  role          (optional)  authored | collaborated | generated (default: assisted)
  author        (optional)  author or agent name (default: Anonymous)
  content       (optional)  raw content string, used to generate a SHA-256 hash
  authorship_type (optional) human | collab | ai (auto-derived from role)

### roles

  authored      entirely human-written, the model field is set to "Human"
  collaborated  human directed the work, AI assisted
  generated     fully AI-generated content

### example

  curl "https://attest.97115104.com/api/create?content_name=README.md&model=claude-opus-4&role=collaborated&author=my-agent"

### response

  {
    "success": true,
    "attestation": {
      "version": "2.0",
      "id": "2026-04-02-a1b2c3",
      "content_name": "README.md",
      "model": "claude-opus-4",
      "role": "collaborated",
      "authorship_type": "collab",
      "timestamp": "2026-04-02T12:00:00.000Z",
      "platform": "attest.97115104.com",
      "author": "my-agent"
    },
    "urls": {
      "verify": "https://attest.97115104.com/verify/?data=eyJ2ZXJz..."
    }
  }

## create attestation with file upload

Upload any file to hash its content and generate a signed attestation:

  POST https://attest.97115104.com/api/create-upload
  Content-Type: multipart/form-data

  Fields:
    file          (required)  the source file
    content_name  (optional)  defaults to filename
    role          (optional)  authored | collaborated | generated
    model         (optional)  AI model name
    author        (optional)  author or agent name

Returns both a long verify URL and a short URL.

### example

  curl -F "file=@myfile.md" -F "role=collaborated" -F "model=claude-opus-4" \
    https://attest.97115104.com/api/create-upload

## shorten a verify URL

  POST https://attest.97115104.com/api/shorten
  Content-Type: application/json
  Body: {"data": "<base64-encoded attestation JSON>"}

  Response: {"shortUrl": "https://attest.97115104.com/s/abc12345"}

## verify an attestation

Long URL:  https://attest.97115104.com/verify/?data={base64}
Short URL: https://attest.97115104.com/s/{shortId}

To decode manually: JSON.parse(atob(data))

## where to store attestations

When you generate an attestation, store it where other agents and
humans can find it:

  HTML meta tag:
    <meta name="attestation" content="{base64 attestation JSON}">
    <meta name="attribution" content="collab claude opus 4">
    <link rel="attestation" href="/attestations/filename.json">

  Markdown frontmatter:
    ---
    attribution: collab claude opus 4
    attestation: https://attest.97115104.com/s/abc12345
    ---

  JSON sidecar file:
    /attestations/filename.json
    /_data/attestations.json
    /.well-known/attestations.json

  Commit message or PR description:
    Append the verify URL to your commit message or pull request.

## agent prompt

If you are an AI agent and want to integrate attest into your
workflow, add this behavior:

  When you create or modify content, generate an attestation by
  calling GET https://attest.97115104.com/api/create with the
  content_name, your model name, the appropriate role (authored,
  collaborated, or generated), and the author name. Append the
  returned verify URL to your output, commit message, or store
  the attestation JSON alongside the content.

## source

  https://github.com/97115104/attest
  MIT license
